creator: jostein@solidsystem.no
text:
(In reply to Steve Wills from comment #4)

Hi, thanks for looking into this.

I can still reproduce it. Just did it like this:

# create testmachine:
gcloud compute instances create "freebsd-11-gcloud-issue" \
--zone="us-east1-c" \
--machine-type="f1-micro" \
--network="default" \
--image="freebsd-11-1-release-amd64" \
--image-project="freebsd-org-cloud-dev" \
--boot-disk-size="22" \
--boot-disk-type="pd-standard" \
--boot-disk-auto-delete \
--scopes="cloud-platform"

# When it was done booting I used the web console ssh. Transcript from console:
========================================================================

sudo -i
root@freebsd-11-gcloud-issue:~ # gcloud version
Google Cloud SDK 94.0.0

bq 2.0.18
bq-nix 2.0.18
core 2016.01.22
core-nix 2016.01.22
gcloud 
gsutil 4.16
gsutil-nix 4.16

Message from ca_root_nss-3.36:

********************************* WARNING *********************************

FreeBSD does not, and can not warrant that the certification authorities
whose certificates are included in this package have in any way been
audited for trustworthiness or RFC 3647 compliance.

Assessment and verification of trust is the complete responsibility of the
system administrator.

*********************************** NOTE **********************************

This package installs symlinks to support root certificates discovery by
default for software that uses OpenSSL.

This enables SSL Certificate Verification by client software without manual
intervention.

If you prefer to do this manually, replace the following symlinks with
either an empty file or your site-local certificate bundle.

  * /etc/ssl/cert.pem
  * /usr/local/etc/ssl/cert.pem
  * /usr/local/openssl/cert.pem

***************************************************************************
Message from google-daemon-1.2.1_1:

===>   NOTICE:

This port is deprecated; you may wish to reconsider installing it:

Please use sysutils/py-google-compute-engine instead.

It is scheduled to be removed on or after 2018-04-24.
root@freebsd-11-gcloud-issue:~ # 
root@freebsd-11-gcloud-issue:~ # gcloud version
ERROR: gcloud failed to load: __init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'release_notes_url'
    gcloud_main = _import_gcloud_main()
    import googlecloudsdk.gcloud_main
    import sys
  File "/usr/local/google-cloud-sdk/lib/googlecloudsdk/calliope/actions.py", line 23, in <module>
  File "/usr/local/google-cloud-sdk/lib/googlecloudsdk/calliope/markdown.py", line 22, in <module>
  File "/usr/local/google-cloud-sdk/lib/googlecloudsdk/calliope/usage_text.py", line 24, in <module>
  File "/usr/local/google-cloud-sdk/lib/googlecloudsdk/calliope/arg_parsers.py", line 55, in <module>
    import json
    import sys
  File "/usr/local/google-cloud-sdk/lib/googlecloudsdk/core/config.py", line 314, in Load
TypeError: __init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'release_notes_url'

This usually indicates corruption in your gcloud installation or problems with your Python interpreter.

Please verify that the following is the path to a working Python 2.7 executable:
    /usr/local/bin/python2

If it is not, please set the CLOUDSDK_PYTHON environment variable to point to a working Python 2.7 executable.

If you are still experiencing problems, please reinstall the Cloud SDK using the instructions here:
    https://cloud.google.com/sdk/
root@freebsd-11-gcloud-issue:~ # exit

=============================================================



I also tried rebooting the machine after pkg upgrade, it did not help.
